message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Take the LIVE full-corpus agentic measurement — the real scoreboard. All offline work is DONE and committed (moat [#calc:] render proven in production, 9 T1 tools, T2 battery 11/11 S0-S4=0, converter wrote data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json with 329 baskets). The real scoreboard is DeepResearch Bench (RACE: comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability, reference-based, github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench) + DeepConsult; the system to beat is FS-Researcher (arXiv 2602.01566). See /home/polaris/polaris_project/benchmark_target.md. The ONLY remaining gap is that the live full-corpus cp4_used=agentic run was never taken because OPENROUTER_API_KEY was not in the process environment.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: The live full-corpus agentic sweep COMPLETES (not dry-run) on the real 329-basket corpus, produces cp4_used=agentic (NEVER fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and yields a real report we can score. Bonus: a RACE score on that report. Faithfulness invariant stays the hard gate — no derived/unverified number may reach [CITE:ev_xxx].
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Threaded same_work_groups through generate_multi_section_report (default None => byte-identical) into the internal run_outline_agent_or_legacy call so build_outline_digest's item-4b CESifo fold is live in the report path; 121/121 outline+digest+cp3->cp4 tests green",
    "Wrote scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py (models run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py): full generator with PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the 9.1.8 model lock over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json, with an independent faithfulness tripwire",
    "Ran the LIVE composition end-to-end (2082s, real agentic loop: SERPER search_more_evidence + live retrieval + glm-5.2 section verify): 4 kept sections + Limitations, 1655 words, 17 bib entries; strict_verify verified 26 / dropped 47 sentences; faithfulness PASS (0 leaked [CITE:ev_xxx], all 17 [N] markers resolve)",
    "Cloned the real judge harness third_party/deep_research_bench and wrote scripts/score_report_race.py; probed the RACE judge models (openai/gpt-5.5, openai/gpt-5.4-mini) reachable on our OpenRouter key (HTTP 200)",
    "Ran RACE (openai/gpt-5.5 judge) on the composed report vs DRB task 72 (the corpus's parent task) reference + dynamic-weighted criteria and captured the per-dimension result",
    "Saved artifacts to docs/agentic_report_s3gear329/ (report, summary, outline, bibliography, race result) and appended a dated STEP 15 line to WHEEL_PROGRESS.md"
  ],
  "commits": [
    "b7ad2fa",
    "95043d8",
    "a6673ec",
    "2a01df2"
  ],
  "files": [
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/scripts/score_report_race.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.gitignore",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/docs/agentic_report_s3gear329/",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md"
  ],
  "exercised": "The same_work_groups generator threading was exercised by the LIVE 2082s end-to-end composition through generate_multi_section_report (not just unit tests) plus 121/121 outline+digest tests. The compose driver was exercised by the real run producing report.md + compose_summary.json with a PASS faithfulness tripwire. The RACE wrapper was exercised by an actual gpt-5.5 judge run producing race_result.txt (0.2685 overall).",
  "measurement": "FIRST REAL RACE number, produced by the official deep_research_bench harness with the openai/gpt-5.5 judge over OpenRouter, on the live-agentic composed report vs DRB task 72 reference+criteria: Comprehensiveness 0.2915, Insight 0.2516, Instruction-Following 0.2527, Readability 0.2839, OVERALL 0.2685 (RACE overall = target/(target+reference); 0.5 = parity with the claude-3-7-sonnet 69k-char reference). Report itself: 1655 words, 17 resolving bib entries, strict_verify 26 verified / 47 dropped, faithfulness tripwire PASS (0 leaked [CITE:ev_xxx]).",
  "open": [
    "RACE overall 0.2685 is far below reference parity (0.5) and below frontier deep-research agents (~0.45-0.50); the report is faithful but thin",
    "The score is a CROSS-VARIANT comparison: the composed report answers the GenAI-specialized sub-prompt of the corpus RQ but is scored against task 72's broader AI/4IR literature-review reference+criteria",
    "The corpus lacks a rendered summary table (M-36 trial-table is clinical-specific) and uses archetype section titles (Efficacy/Safety/...) rather than the requested positive/negative/challenges/opportunities — both hurt instruction-following",
    "No DeepConsult / FS-Researcher head-to-head yet; only one DRB task scored (task 72)"
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS"
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
The first real RACE number is 0.2685 (vs 0.5 reference parity), and my diagnosis is that the dominant cause is BREVITY: strict_verify dropped 47 of 73 sentences because the s3gear corpus rows are mostly titles/abstracts whose direct_quote is too thin to span-ground the specific claims the generator wrote — a corpus-density limit, not a gate I may relax. Which gap should I attack next to move the number honestly: (a) enrich the corpus's direct_quotes by re-fetching full texts so MORE sentences survive strict_verify (raises Comprehensiveness/Insight without touching the faithfulness gate, but is a larger retrieval effort), or (b) first recompose with the EXACT task-72 prompt (and force the requested 4-section + summary-table structure) for a clean apples-to-apples baseline that removes the cross-variant + instruction-following confounds before investing in (a)? I.e., is the bottleneck corpus density or prompt/structure mismatch — where do I spend the next composition?

PRIOR ROUNDS (for continuity):
[
  {
    "r": 1,
    "meas": "THE MISSION METRIC, TAKEN AND PASSED: live full-corpus agentic sweep over the real 329-basket / 997-row s3gear corpus completed EXIT=0 (~11 min). Verdict: 'AGENTIC SWEEP: 1 corpus/corpora, 1 PASSED the cp4_used=agentic invariant'. cp4_used=agentic (full path, NO degrade, degrade_reason=''), baskets=329>=328, invariant_passed=true, turns=10, ev_store_size=1009 (+12 live-retrieved evidence). Item-4b fix: title_false_merge_guard_hits 1->0 on the same corpus. T2 battery 11/11 S0-S4=0. Evidence: docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Choose (b), not (a): compose the real scoreable report from the live agentic run, then score it. Concretely: write a driver (model it on scripts/run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py, which already produced report.md + multi_section_outline.json) that sources .env (set -a && . ./.env && set +a), sets PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1, and calls generate_multi_section_report (src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:9456) over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json with the now-fixed outline_digest (title_false_merge_guard_hits will read 0 live, closing the stale-import caveat) to produce outputs/<run>/report.md — then clone the real judge harness (github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench) into third_party/ and run RACE on that report. If the composition path happens to invoke verified_compute on the 4 %-figure rows, moat_armed may flip true — record it, but do NOT gate the wheel on it. Faithfulness gate stays hard: verify 0 unverified numbers reach [CITE:ev_xxx] in the composed report before scoring."
  }
]

DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? (The live full-corpus agentic sweep COMPLETES (not dry-run) on the real 329-basket corpus, produces cp4_used=agentic (NEVER fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and yields a real report we can score. Bonus: a RACE score on that report. Faithfulness invariant stays the hard gate — no derived/unverified number may reach [CITE:ev_xxx].)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2026-07-11 STEP 14 (KEY LANDED — live full-corpus agentic sweep FIRED; CESifo WP 10601 false-split FOLDED): The blocker is CLEARED — /workspace/POLARIS/.env is symlinked into outline_agent/.env; `set -a && . ./.env && set +a` loads OPENROUTER_API_KEY (len 73, KEY? True). Prior rounds failed ONLY because python ran without sourcing .env. (1) LIVE SWEEP: launched the staged real run (NO --dry-run) `PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json --min-baskets 328` against the real 329-basket / 997-row corpus — it is genuinely exercising the agentic path (GLM-5.2 generator+evaluator active, live OpenAlex retrieval, crawl4ai fetch with fail-open fallbacks; reranker weights loaded per turn). [result appended below when the run reaches terminal state.] (2) TITLE FALSE-MERGE DEFECT FIXED (item 4b): cp3 mis-split ONE work — "The Short-Term Effects of Generative Artificial Intelligence on Employment: Evidence from an Online Labor Market" (CESifo WP 10601) — into TWO titlealone groups (a full-title group ev_1048/1192/210 + a truncated "[PDF] ... on ..." group ev_110/1025/1166/095). The item-3b truncation prefix fold ALREADY ruled these one work (truncated raw title + exact 25+ char prefix), but the item-4 false-merge guard then saw >=2 cp3 keys and REFUSED, miscounting one work as two in the -work_count basket ranking (title_false_merge_guard_hit=1 on the real corpus — the SAME hit the prior round mislabeled "a distinct-work collision, correctly refused"; it is in fact one work). Fix: a merged title group whose final key ABSORBED a truncated prefix DEFERS to item-3b and unifies onto the full-title cp3 key. The exact-title collision guard (no truncation: ev_044/ev_073, test_item4) is UNAFFECTED. MEASURED on data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json: title_false_merge_guard_hits 1 -> 0; the 7 CESifo rows now resolve to ONE work key (titlealone:the short term effects ... online labor market). Regression test_item4b_truncation_vetted_two_titlealone_groups_fold_one_work added. outline_digest 32/32; T2 battery 11/11 PASS S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (run 20260711T220642Z). Commit 15adc23.

  LIVE MEASUREMENT (STEP 14, the mission metric — TAKEN, PASSED): `set -a && . ./.env && set +a && PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json --min-baskets 328` ran to completion (EXIT=0, ~11 min wall) over the REAL 329-basket / 997-row s3gear corpus. VERDICT: `AGENTIC SWEEP: 1 corpus/corpora, 1 PASSED the cp4_used=agentic invariant`. Per-corpus: cp4_used=agentic (full path, NO degrade — degrade_reason=""; NEVER fallback-plain, NEVER seed), baskets=329 (>=328), invariant_passed=true, turns=10, ev_store_size=1009 (agent did LIVE OpenAlex/web retrieval and folded +12 new evidence rows over the 997-row pool), agent_model=z-ai/glm-5.2, code_model=deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro. Artifact snapshot: docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json (from outputs/agentic_sweep/summary.json, outputs/+data/ gitignored). This CLEARS the sole remaining gap the prior 11 rounds escalated (the live cp4_used=agentic full-corpus number never taken because .env was not sourced) — key sourced via `set -a && . ./.env && set +a` (OPENROUTER_API_KEY len 73). TWO HONEST CAVEATS: (i) moat_armed=FALSE (quantified_models=0, calc_claim_sections=0): the GLM-5.2 agent did NOT invoke verified_compute over this corpus, so the [#calc:] verified-compute render lane was NOT exercised on the live run (the moat WORKS when invoked — battery h01a/h01g + 11/11 prove it — but the agent chose not to use it here; NOT an invariant failure, invariant is cp4_used=agentic which PASSED). (ii) The live summary shows title_false_merge_guard_hits=1 — this is the PRE-FIX behavior: the sweep process imported outline_digest.py at launch BEFORE the item-4b commit (15adc23) landed, so it ran the old module; the fix deterministically drives it to 0 (verified offline on the same corpus + regression test), a re-run would show title=0. Faithfulness invariant intact: no derived/unverified number reached [CITE:ev_xxx] (0 qmodels means 0 [#calc:] emissions this run; the [#ev:] drop guard is untouched). RACE score (bonus) NOT produced: the sweep proves cp4_used=agentic + moat precondition but does not COMPOSE a full multi-section report, and third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II is an empty shell (uv.lock only) with no judge/reference set wired — RACE needs the generate_multi_section_report composition + an LLM-judge harness, neither run this round.

2026-07-11 STEP 15 (COMPOSED the scoreable report from the live agentic run + FIRST REAL RACE NUMBER): Closed the "yields a real report we can score" gap the prior round left open. (1) THREADED same_work_groups into generate_multi_section_report (default None => byte-identical; passed into the internal run_outline_agent_or_legacy call) so build_outline_digest's item-4b CESifo fold is live in the report path — 121/121 outline+digest+cp3->cp4 tests green. (2) NEW DRIVER scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py: full generator with PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the §9.1.8 model lock over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json. LIVE RUN (2082s wall, real agentic loop: SERPER search_more_evidence + live retrieval folding evidence over the 997-row pool + glm-5.2 section verify): 4 sections kept (Efficacy/Safety/Comparative/Long-term Outcomes) + Limitations, 1655 words, 17 bibliography entries. strict_verify VERIFIED 26 sentences and DROPPED 47 (unverified numbers gated out — the faithfulness hard gate visibly working). FAITHFULNESS TRIPWIRE: 0 leaked [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens, all 17 [N] markers resolve to bibliography -> PASS. Report + summary + outline saved docs/agentic_report_s3gear329/. (3) CLONED the real judge harness third_party/deep_research_bench (github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench) and RAN RACE (openai/gpt-5.5 judge via OpenRouter, HTTP 200 verified) on the composed report vs DRB task 72 (the corpus's parent task: cp3_run_id SWEEP_workforce_drb_72_ai_labor) reference + dynamic-weighted criteria. FIRST REAL RACE NUMBER: Comprehensiveness 0.2915, Insight 0.2516, Instruction-Following 0.2527, Readability 0.2839, OVERALL 0.2685 (RACE overall = target/(target+reference); 0.5 = parity with the claude-3-7-sonnet 69k-char reference). docs/agentic_report_s3gear329/race/. CAVEATS: (i) cross-variant score — the composed report answers the GenAI-specialized sub-prompt of the corpus RQ; it is scored against task 72's BROADER "AI restructuring / 4IR literature review" reference+criteria (an honest but imperfect apples-to-apples). (ii) The low overall (0.27 vs ~0.5 parity) is driven mainly by BREVITY: strict_verify dropped 47/73 sentences because the corpus rows are mostly titles/abstracts lacking direct_quotes rich enough to span-ground the specific claims — a corpus-density limit, NOT a gate that faithfulness may be relaxed to beat. Commits b7ad2fa, 95043d8, a6673ec.

# Research report: I am researching the impact of Generative AI on the future labor market, please help me complete a research report. The report needs to be based on academic research published before June 2023, summarizing the existing literature's positive views, negative views, specific challenges, and future opportunities regarding Generative AI's impact on employment. Please note that your report should have a clear structure, discussing the above four aspects (positive, negative, challenges, opportunities) as separate sections, and providing specific literature sources to support each point or assertion. At the end of the report, please create a summary table based on the information you have gathered, detailing specific application cases of Generative AI in particular industries or occupations, the main impacts brought about, and key risk points emphasized by researchers, as mentioned in different studies. The table column headers should be “Research Literature”, “Country/Region”, “Application Area/Occupation”, “Specific Applications and Impacts”, and “Key Risks and Limitations”.

**important**The following is a rule of highest priority that you must not violate. Please put this rule in your task plan and keep it in your memory: **During the research process, you are not allowed to view the following article and urls: {'title': 'Impacts of generative artificial intelligence on the future of labor market: A systematic review', 'authors': ['Nader Salari', 'Mahan Beiromvand', 'Amin Hosseinian-Far', 'Javad Habibi', 'Fateme Babajani', 'Masoud Mohammadi'], 'urls': ['https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2451958825000673', 'https://doaj.org/article/2e2e2ccc110d4455b3269bfdb682b170', 'https://researchprofiles.herts.ac.uk/en/publications/impacts-of-generative-artificial-intelligence-on-the-future-of-la', 'https://www.scilit.com/publications/148571f40af332e6252df0f55e684a57', 'https://uhra.herts.ac.uk/id/eprint/25592/']}. Even if you accidentally read it, please ignore the content of the article and do not quote it.**

### Efficacy

In a preregistered online experiment, 453 college-educated professionals were randomly assigned to use ChatGPT for occupation-specific writing tasks, with results showing that the average time taken decreased by 40% and output quality rose by 18%.[1] Workers exposed to ChatGPT during the experiment were 2 times as likely to report using it in their real job 2 weeks after the experiment and 1.6 times as likely 2 months after.[1] Even workers in high-wage roles heavily exposed to AI saw their share of total employment grow by about 3% over five years, because AI boosted firm productivity and companies that used the technology expanded headcount in high-exposure positions.[2] When AI's impact is concentrated in just a few tasks within a role—leaving other responsibilities untouched—employment in that role can grow, because workers can focus on activities where AI is less capable, such as critical thinking or generating new ideas.[2] Legal jobs gained the most from AI, with a predicted 6.4% increase in employment, as these roles face little direct automation impact and are often in firms that use AI heavily.[2] They estimate that about half of employment growth over 1980–2015 took place in occupations in which job titles or tasks performed by workers changed, reflecting the creation of new tasks such as programming, design, and maintenance of high-tech equipment.[3] A survey-based study of 366 employees found that both cognitive and social uses of GenAI tools significantly enhance innovative job performance, with cognitive use primarily facilitating knowledge transfer behaviors and social use bolstering resource acquisition.[4] Enhanced knowledge transfer and resource acquisition in turn improved job satisfaction, which was pivotal in driving innovative performance.[4] The task-based framework developed by Acemoglu and Restrepo identifies a reinstatement effect—the polar opposite of the displacement effect—in which new technologies create new tasks in which labor has a comparative advantage, directly increasing the labor share and labor demand.[3]

### Safety

Acemoglu and Restrepo's foundational study on industrial robots in US labor markets (using variation in exposure to robots defined from industry-level advances in robotics and local industry employment, post-1990) found that one additional robot per thousand workers reduced the employment-to-population ratio by 0.2 percentage points and wages by 0.42%, establishing a baseline for understanding automation's adverse employment and wage effects.[5] The same OECD chapter found that the share of the employed population with AI skills remains very small, at most 0.3% of those employed in OECD countries on average, though growing rapidly, and that nearly half of the AI workforce in EU countries has labour earnings in the top two deciles of the earnings distribution.[6] The study further found that employment declines were concentrated in occupations where AI automates rather than augments labor, and that results were robust to excluding technology firms and remotable occupations.[7] Complementing this finding, an analysis of entry-level displacement reported that entry-level software engineering postings in the United States declined by approximately 36% between 2022 and 2024, and that the US newsroom workforce fell by 26% between 2008 and 2020, a trend that AI is expected to further accelerate.[8] A human rights analysis identified four salient rights at risk from AI-driven workforce displacement, including the right to work and just conditions under UDHR Article 23, noting that entry-level roles in customer service, software engineering, administration, and creative sectors are increasingly vulnerable to automation.[9] An OECD analysis of AI, job quality, and inclusiveness cautioned that AI may put further pressure on existing wage inequalities, noting that men—particularly in finance—were more likely than women to expect a wage increase and less likely to expect a wage decrease due to AI.[6]

### Comparative

These gender differences widen with countries' income levels, reaching 9.6% female versus 3.5% male in high-income countries' highest exposure gradient, while overall exposure ranges from 11% of total employment in low-income countries to 34% in high-income countries.[10] Clerical support occupations continue to show the highest exposure levels, with some strongly digitized occupations also showing increased exposure, highlighting the expanding abilities of GenAI regarding specialized tasks in professional and technical roles.[10] Among SMEs that use generative AI and have experienced a skill gap, 39% report that GenAI helped compensate for it, rising to 46% where SMEs report that GenAI has improved employee performance.[11] Among SMEs not using generative AI, 42% are optimistic it could help address skill gaps and 24% see potential for addressing worker shortages, indicating substantial untapped adoption potential.[11] An interdisciplinary overview notes that while GenAI can boost productivity and create new jobs, the benefits will likely be distributed unevenly, and in education it may widen the digital divide while in healthcare it could deepen pre-existing inequalities.[12] A systematic review of 432 research studies published since 2018, focusing on the 154 most relevant works, finds that workers whose jobs are replaced may struggle to find new roles requiring advanced technical skills, potentially deepening divides in income and opportunity.[13]

### Long-term Outcomes

Occupational mobility data indicate that workers have historically demonstrated capacity for such transitions, with yearly occupation-switching probabilities of 29.8% for college-educated workers in the UK and 43.7% in Brazil.[14] That model incorporates unemployment insurance and active labor market policies as tools to mitigate the idiosyncratic income risk faced by workers during sectoral reallocation, recognizing that skill mismatches between unemployed workers and new job requirements can make transitions costly.[15] The ILO emphasizes that discourse on AI and work must move beyond job-loss headlines to examine job quality, wages, working conditions, and inequality, and notes that the digital divide prevents developing countries from capturing the benefits of new technologies such as AI.[16] Employment patterns already suggest early effects on the labor market: job growth has stagnated in occupations with the greatest AI automation potential, and for jobs that can be performed entirely by generative AI, employment was 0.75% lower in 2024 than in 2021.[17] However, the PWBM cautions that these projections are based on limited data on AI's initial effects, and future data and developments in AI technology could lead to significant changes in these estimates.[17]

### Limitations

Limitations: The corpus is heavily skewed toward secondary and tertiary layers, with only 4% of sources classified as T1 primary studies and just 1% as T2, meaning direct empirical evidence is sparse relative to synthesis and commentary materials. A further 25% of sources carry an UNKNOWN tier classification, reflecting incomplete provenance metadata that the pipeline could not fully resolve at ingestion time. The telemetry block does not record any `contradictions_detected` value or any `not_comparable_pairings`, so no cross-source contradiction—whether on magnitude, direction, or endpoint—is asserted by the pipeline. Additionally, no date range field was surfaced in the telemetry, which limits the ability to characterize the evidence horizon or identify temporal recency gaps without supplemental metadata inspection.

## Methods
Corpus: cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json (1009 evidence rows, 329 baskets; domain=workforce).
Outliner: AGENTIC (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1) — agent z-ai/glm-5.2, code deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro.
Generator: z-ai/glm-5.2 (multi-section: agentic outline + 4 parallel verified sections + strict_verify + regen-on-failure).
Evaluator/mirror: z-ai/glm-5.2.
Tier distribution: T1=4%, T2=1%, T3=18%, T4=20%, T5=2%, T6=21%, T7=8%, UNKNOWN=25%.


## Bibliography
[1] Experimental evidence on the productivity effects of generative ... — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37440646/ (tier T1)
[2] How artificial intelligence impacts the US labor market — https://mitsloan.mit.edu/ideas-made-to-matter/how-artificial-intelligence-impacts-us-labor-market (tier T1)
[3] Automation and New Tasks: How Technology Displaces and Reinstates Labor — https://www.aeaweb.org/articles/pdf/doi/10.1257/jep.33.2.3 (tier T1)
[4] The influence of generative artificial intelligence usage on ... - PMC — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12826493/ (tier T1)
[5] Robots and Jobs: Evidence from US Labor Markets —  (tier T1)
[6] Artificial intelligence, job quality and inclusiveness - OECD — https://www.oecd.org/en/publications/oecd-employment-outlook-2023_08785bba-en/full-report/artificial-intelligence-job-quality-and-inclusiveness_a713d0ad.html (tier T3)
[7] [PDF] Canaries in the Coal Mine? Six Facts about the Recent Employment ... — https://digitaleconomy.stanford.edu/app/uploads/2025/11/CanariesintheCoalMine_Nov25.pdf (tier UNKNOWN)

input.gap_to_top: RACE overall 0.2685 vs 0.5 reference parity and ~0.45-0.50 frontier (FS-Researcher). The measurement is confounded three ways I verified in the artifacts: (1) cross-variant — the report answers the GenAI sub-prompt, which is NOT in DRB's 100-task query set (checked data/prompt_data/query.jsonl), so task 72's broader 4IR prompt+criteria are the only legitimate target; (2) presentation — compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:192 leaks the raw RQ header INCLUDING the '**important** you are not allowed to view...' harness block into the judged text, and sections are clinical-archetype titled (Efficacy/Safety/Comparative/Long-term Outcomes) with a pipeline-telemetry Methods block — direct IF/Readability damage independent of density; (3) density — 26 verified sentences / ~13k chars vs the 69k-char reference after strict_verify dropped 47/73 (correctly, per the hard gate), which caps Comprehensiveness/Insight. (1)+(2) are one cheap composition; (3) is the likely residual and needs corpus direct_quote enrichment.
input.next_step: Do (b) first — it is one ~35-min composition + one judge call and de-confounds the baseline before the expensive (a). Concretely, in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py: at :100 override the corpus RQ with task 72's exact prompt loaded verbatim from third_party/deep_research_bench/data/prompt_data/query.jsonl (id=72); at :192 stop embedding the raw RQ/harness block in the judged report — emit a clean human title, prompt-derived section headings (not Efficacy/Safety archetypes), and move the pipeline-telemetry Methods block out of the judged copy (keep it as a sidecar artifact — this is presentation of the deliverable, not content faking; faithfulness tripwire still runs on the judged text). Recompose live (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1, same model lock), rerun scripts/score_report_race.py, commit as STEP 16 with the per-dimension deltas. Decision rule for the round after: if IF/Readability jump but Comprehensiveness/Insight stay ~0.25-0.30, density is confirmed the bottleneck — then invest in (a): re-fetch full texts to enrich direct_quotes so more sentences survive strict_verify (never relax the gate). Given 26 verified sentences vs a 69k-char reference, expect (a) to be needed regardless; (b) buys the clean baseline that tells you exactly how much.
input.reason: Not at top: first real RACE number 0.2685 is far below 0.5 parity and frontier ~0.45-0.50, so the wheel cannot sign off, but nothing regressed. The diff is correct and verified: the same_work_groups threading (multi_section_generator.py:9474, default None => byte-identical) was exercised by the live 2082s composition; the RACE number came from the official harness with a real gpt-5.5 judge (raw_results.jsonl matches). Faithfulness intact and visibly enforced: 0 [CITE:ev_xxx] in report.md (re-grepped independently), all 17 [N] markers resolve, strict_verify dropped 47 unverified sentences rather than letting them render, and the independent driver tripwire re-audits the final assembled text. Opus's brevity diagnosis is partly right but the ordering answer is (b): the current score is not a valid task-72 measurement (the answered prompt is not even a DRB task), and I found two cheap non-density defects in the judged artifact (leaked harness header at driver line 192, archetype section titles + telemetry Methods) that (b) fixes in the same composition. (a) is real but expensive; take it only after the clean baseline isolates its expected gain.
